documents.title: UK Student Route Visa — Official Requirements
documents.summary: The Student Route (formerly Tier 4) is the primary visa for international students at UK universities. It requires a Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies (CAS) from a UKVI-licensed sponsor and proof of financial maintenance. From July 2025, visa decisions are issued as a digital eVisa through a UKVI account rather than a physical BRP sticker.

scope.failureImpact: Visa refusal, curtailment of leave, removal from UK, scholarship revocation

timeline.T-6 months: Earliest application date before course start
timeline.Within 7 days (arrival): Police registration if visa requires it; address registration with sponsor

details: You must have a valid CAS reference number from a UKVI-licensed Student sponsor before applying
CAS contains your course details, tuition fees paid, and sponsor's licence number
CAS is valid for 6 months — apply within this window
One CAS per application; changing institution after visa grant requires a new visa in most cases
Check your sponsor appears on the UKVI Register of Licensed Sponsors with 'track record' status, which affects your work rights

commonViolations.text: Beginning studies before visa is granted
commonViolations.consequence: Working or studying without leave — status violation, removal risk

commonViolations.text: Changing to a different course or institution without a new visa
commonViolations.consequence: Breach of visa conditions, curtailment of leave

controls: Confirm CAS reference number before applying
Verify your sponsor's 'track record' status on the UKVI register (affects work hour limit)
Apply no earlier than 6 months before your course start date

details: London institutions: £1,483 per month for up to 9 months (maximum £13,347)
Outside London: £1,136 per month for up to 9 months (maximum £10,224)
Funds must have been held for at least 28 consecutive days
The 28-day period must end within 31 days of your visa application date
If your CAS shows tuition fees already paid, you may not need to show the full amount
MoHE sponsorship letters are accepted as evidence of financial support
failureModes: Funds held for fewer than 28 days
28-day period ending more than 31 days before application
Using joint accounts or funds not in your name without declaration
mitigation: Prepare bank statements or official MoHE sponsorship letter well in advance
Check gov.uk/student-visa/money for the most current monthly figures
If MoHE covers living costs, obtain an official letter confirming the monthly allowance amount

details: From 15 July 2025, Student Route decisions are issued as a digital eVisa via your UKVI account — not as a physical sticker (vignette) in your passport
Create a UKVI account and link your eVisa before travel
Share your eVisa with airlines and employers using the online share code service at gov.uk/view-prove-immigration-status
Physical BRP cards are no longer issued for Student Route grants
controls: Register for a UKVI account immediately after visa grant
Save your share code before each international trip
Report any issues with your eVisa to UKVI before travel

details: Some nationalities are required to register with the UK police within 7 days of arriving
Check your visa conditions — if required, it will be stated on your entry clearance/eVisa
Registration is done at the Overseas Visitor Records Office (OVRO) at the local police station
Omani nationals should verify at the time of visa grant whether this condition applies
mitigation: Check your visa conditions immediately upon receipt
If required, register within 7 days of arrival — do not delay

details: You must study at the institution and on the course specified in your CAS
Sponsor monitors attendance and must report significant absences to UKVI
If you withdraw from or are excluded from your course, your sponsor must notify UKVI and your visa may be curtailed to 60 days
Changing course level downward (e.g., master's to bachelor's) triggers sponsor reporting duties

commonViolations.consequence: Sponsor reports to UKVI, visa curtailed to 60 days
UKVI notification, leave curtailed, overstay risk

UKVI_Contact_Centre.name: UK Visas and Immigration
UKVI_Contact_Centre.title: Student visa enquiries
UKVI_Contact_Centre.description: For visa application queries and account issues

UKCISA.name: UK Council for International Student Affairs
UKCISA.title: Independent advice for international students
UKCISA.description: Free independent advice on UK immigration rules for students

links.title: Student Visa — GOV.UK
links.description: Official overview, application, and eligibility

links.title: Financial Evidence Guidance — GOV.UK
links.description: Detailed rules on acceptable financial evidence

links.title: Register of Licensed Student Sponsors
links.description: Check your university's sponsor status and track record rating

links.title: UKCISA — Student Route Eligibility
links.description: Plain-English guide to Student Route requirements

complianceNotes: MoHE-sponsored students must obtain MoHE written approval before transferring to a different UK institution
Any change in enrolment status must be reported to both your sponsor and MoHE
Financial requirements are reviewed annually — check gov.uk for current figures before applying
Your sponsor's 'track record' status determines whether you can work 20 hrs/week — verify before accepting employment

documents.title: UK Student Route — Work Rights and Restrictions
documents.summary: Students on the Student Route may work during term time within strict hour limits and without restriction during vacations. Work rights depend on your sponsor's UKVI 'track record' status and your course level. Certain types of work are always prohibited.

appliesTo: Omani students on Student Route visa
scope.failureImpact: Visa curtailment, removal from UK, scholarship revocation, future visa refusals

details: Degree level or above (e.g., bachelor's, master's, PhD) at a sponsor with 'track record' status: maximum 20 hours per week during term
Below degree level (e.g., A-levels, foundation): maximum 10 hours per week during term
If your sponsor does NOT have 'track record' status: you may have no right to work during term — check your visa conditions
The week runs Monday to Sunday; any combination of jobs must not exceed your weekly limit
You may not work before your course start date

commonViolations.text: Working more than 20 hours/week during term time
commonViolations.consequence: Breach of visa conditions, visa curtailment, possible removal

commonViolations.text: Working for a sponsor that does not have track record status when your visa only permits work at a track-record sponsor

mitigation: Verify your sponsor's track record status on the UKVI register before accepting any job
Keep payslips and timesheets to demonstrate compliance if audited
Consult your International Student Support team before accepting any employment

details: During official term vacations: no weekly hour limit — you may work full time
Also applies: before your course starts (from visa grant), and during any study break authorized by your sponsor
After your course ends: you enter a grace period (see UK-GRACE-PERIOD-2026) — you may still work if permitted by your visa conditions
Self-employment and running your own business are not permitted on the Student Route
Work as a professional sportsperson (including a sports coach) is not permitted
Work as a professional entertainer is not permitted
Unpaid internships that constitute work for immigration purposes may still count toward your hours limit
Volunteering for a charity is generally allowed and does not count toward the hours limit, but do not displace paid workers
failureModes: Freelancing or contracting through a personal company
Accepting unpaid internships that exceed your hours allowance

details: MoHE scholarship terms may restrict or prohibit employment — check your scholarship contract
Any employment income must be reported to MoHE annually
Obtaining a National Insurance (NI) number is required for employment; apply through the UK government (gov.uk/apply-national-insurance-number)

links.title: Working during your Student visa — GOV.UK
links.description: Official guidance on work rights for students

links.title: UKCISA — Student Work
links.description: Plain-English explanation of work restrictions

links.title: Apply for a National Insurance Number
links.description: Required before starting any paid employment in the UK

documents.title: UK Graduate Route (Post-Study Work Visa)
documents.summary: The Graduate Route allows eligible graduates of UK universities to remain in the UK to work or look for work after completing their studies. It does not require employer sponsorship. Duration is 2 years for bachelor's and master's graduates (reducing to 18 months from 1 January 2027) and 3 years for PhD graduates.

appliesTo: Omani students who have completed a UK degree
scope.failureImpact: Unable to remain legally in UK; MoHE return obligation triggers if application not planned properly

timeline.T-0: Apply before current Student visa expires
timeline.After course completion confirmed: Sponsor notifies Home Office; application window opens

details: Must have completed an eligible UK bachelor's, master's, or doctoral degree at a licensed Student sponsor
Must apply before your Student Route visa expires
Your sponsor must have notified the Home Office that you successfully completed your course
Must be in the UK when you apply
No minimum salary or job offer required
Bachelor's or master's graduates: 2 years (IMPORTANT: reducing to 18 months for applications made on or after 1 January 2027)
PhD or doctoral graduates: 3 years (unchanged)
CRITICAL for MoHE scholars: Your scholarship contract requires return to Oman within 60 days of programme completion — applying for the Graduate Route does NOT override this MoHE obligation
You must obtain prior MoHE written approval before applying for the Graduate Route; failure to return may trigger full repayment obligations
failureModes: Applying for Graduate Route without MoHE approval and then being required to repay scholarship funds
Relying on 2-year grant without awareness of the January 2027 reduction to 18 months
mitigation: Contact MoHE Scholarship Office 6 months before graduation to discuss post-completion options
Get MoHE approval in writing before filing Graduate Route application
Check gov.uk/graduate-visa for the latest grant duration before applying

details: Can work in any role, at any skill level — no restriction on occupation
Can be self-employed or run a business (unlike the Student Route)
No weekly hour limit
Can switch to Skilled Worker route if employer offers sponsorship

links.title: Graduate Visa — GOV.UK
links.description: Official eligibility, application, and conditions

links.title: UKCISA — Working After Studies
links.description: Guidance on Graduate Route and other post-study options

complianceNotes: MoHE approval is required before applying — do not assume the Graduate Route is compatible with your scholarship contract without written MoHE confirmation
The Graduate Route does not lead directly to settlement (Indefinite Leave to Remain) — further applications are required
Tax on UK employment income is mandatory; register with HMRC (gov.uk/register-for-self-assessment if self-employed)

documents.title: Academic Technology Approval Scheme (ATAS)
documents.summary: ATAS clearance is required before studying or researching in certain sensitive subject areas at UK universities. It is issued by the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) and must be obtained before your visa can be granted and before starting the relevant course. New applicant guidance was released 8 January 2025.

appliesTo: Postgraduate research students in sensitive STEM fields
Some taught master's students in sensitive fields
scope.failureImpact: Visa refusal, inability to start course, scholarship interruption

details: Required for students from non-exempt countries studying certain subjects at postgraduate level — primarily STEM, dual-use, and defence-sensitive fields
Your university will provide a CAH3 code and confirm whether ATAS is needed for your specific course
Omani nationals are not on the exempt list (exempt list includes EU nationals and a small number of others) — you must apply if your course requires it
Need a separate ATAS certificate for each institution and each programme of study
Apply online via the ATAS portal: academic-technology-approval.service.gov.uk
Certificate is valid for 6 months from issue date for the purpose of a visa application
Must apply early — processing can take several weeks; allow at least 4-6 weeks before your visa application
If you change institution or course, you need a new ATAS certificate
failureModes: Applying too late and missing visa application window
Changing research topic substantially without obtaining new ATAS clearance
controls: Ask your admissions team whether your course requires ATAS before accepting your offer
Apply for ATAS as soon as you receive your CAH3 code — do not wait for CAS
Build at least 6 weeks for ATAS processing into your pre-arrival timeline

links.title: ATAS — GOV.UK
links.description: Official ATAS guidance, subject lists, and application portal

links.title: Find out if you need an ATAS certificate — GOV.UK
links.description: Subject-by-subject checker

documents.title: Maintaining Student Route Status — UK
documents.summary: Maintaining valid Student Route immigration status in the UK requires active compliance with course attendance, timely reporting of personal information changes, and understanding grace periods. Failure to maintain status can result in curtailment of leave to 60 days.

appliesTo: All Omani students on Student Route visa in UK
scope.failureImpact: Leave curtailed to 60 days, removal proceedings, future visa refusal, scholarship termination

timeline.Within 7 days of arrival: Register address with sponsor; police registration if required by visa
timeline.Before travel abroad: Confirm eVisa is accessible; no specific travel signature needed (unlike F-1 I-20)
timeline.60 days before leave expiry: Apply to extend if course continues beyond current visa
timeline.Post-completion: 60-day grace period to depart, switch status, or apply for Graduate Route

details: Attend all scheduled lectures, seminars, tutorials, and supervisor meetings as required by your sponsor
Your sponsor is legally required to monitor your attendance and report significant unauthorized absences to UKVI
If you withdraw or are excluded, your sponsor must report this to UKVI — your visa may be curtailed to 60 days from the report date
Postgraduate research students (dissertations, theses) are still monitored throughout their research period
mitigation: Notify your department and International Student Support team of any medical or personal issues before missing contact points
Keep a record of authorized absences and supervisor correspondence

details: Report your UK address to your sponsor within 7 days of arrival or moving
Address changes must be updated in your university student record system
Name or passport changes must be reported to your sponsor and UKVI promptly
Update your UKVI account with any changes to personal details
If your course continues beyond your current visa expiry, you must apply to extend before the current visa expires
Apply online via UKVI; you will need a new CAS from your sponsor
There is no automatic extension — do not let your leave expire
MoHE approval may be required for an extension if it extends total program duration beyond the original scholarship term
failureModes: Allowing leave to expire before extension is granted — unlawful presence begins
Applying without a valid CAS — application will be refused

details: Upon successful completion of your course, you have a short period (typically until your visa expiry date, or the period stated on your visa) to remain in the UK
Use this time to apply for the Graduate Route, switch to another visa, or depart
Do NOT confuse UK visa expiry with course end date — check your eVisa for your exact leave expiry date

International_Student_Support.name: International Student Support Team
International_Student_Support.title: Immigration and compliance advice
International_Student_Support.description: Available at all UKVI-licensed universities — check your university website for contact details

links.title: Protecting your Student status — UKCISA
links.description: Practical guide to avoiding status violations

links.title: Extend Student Visa — GOV.UK
links.description: Official extension application process

documents.title: Healthcare in the UK — NHS and Immigration Health Surcharge
documents.summary: Most Student Route visa holders access UK healthcare through the National Health Service (NHS) after paying the Immigration Health Surcharge (IHS) as part of their visa application. Unlike the US, you are not required to purchase private insurance — the IHS covers most NHS treatment. Some services carry charges.

appliesTo: All Omani students on Student Route visa in UK
scope.failureImpact: Unexpected medical costs if IHS not paid; visa refusal if IHS payment incomplete

details: Paid upfront as part of your visa application — you cannot apply without paying it

Current rate for students: £776 per year (as of 2025) — confirm the exact amount at gov.uk/healthcare-immigration-application/how-much-pay before applying, as the rate is updated by Parliament and can change
The IHS is calculated based on the full length of your visa — for a 3-year Student Route visa the total IHS is approximately £2,328; for a 4-year visa approximately £3,104
Covers most NHS hospital and GP treatment at the same cost as UK residents — but does NOT make all healthcare free
IHS does NOT cover: routine dental treatment, optician services (eye tests, glasses, contact lenses), or some specialist treatments — these require separate private payment

IHS does NOT make NHS prescriptions free — in England, each prescription item carries a flat charge (see NHS Prescription Charges rule below); prescriptions are free in Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland
MoHE-sponsored students should check whether MoHE reimburses IHS costs — obtain written confirmation from your scholarship officer before applying
controls: Pay IHS in full as part of the visa application — partial payment causes refusal
Keep your IHS payment reference number — you may need it when registering with a GP
Check gov.uk/healthcare-immigration-application/how-much-pay for the confirmed rate before applying

details: In England, each NHS prescription item carries a flat charge of £9.90 per item (2024/25 rate, reviewed annually each April — check nhs.uk for the current charge)

If you take multiple regular medications, a Prescription Prepayment Certificate (PPC) may save money: a 3-month PPC (approximately £32) covers unlimited prescriptions for that period; a 12-month PPC (approximately £111) covers the full year
Prescriptions are FREE in Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland — if you study outside England, you will not pay prescription charges
You may be exempt from charges in England if you have certain qualifying medical conditions — ask your GP or pharmacist whether you qualify
Controlled drugs and some specialist medicines may have different dispensing rules — bring a 3-month supply of any chronic medications from Oman with a doctor's note and customs declaration
The IHS does NOT make NHS dental treatment free — routine NHS dentistry carries patient charges in England even when you have paid the IHS

NHS England dental charges are banded (2024/25 rates, reviewed annually): Band 1 — £26.80 (check-up, diagnosis, scale and polish, preventive treatment); Band 2 — £73.50 (fillings, root canal treatment, extractions); Band 3 — £319.10 (crowns, dentures, bridges, more complex restorative work)
These are per-course-of-treatment charges, not per appointment — one Band 2 charge covers all treatment within that course regardless of how many appointments it takes
Scotland: NHS dental treatment is free for all patients; Wales and Northern Ireland: reduced or no charges — confirm with your specific provider if studying outside England

Emergency dental treatment in England: a Band 1 charge (£26.80) covers immediate pain relief or emergency treatment at an NHS dentist or walk-in dental clinic; call NHS 111 to find an emergency dentist
Private dental treatment has no fixed pricing — costs vary significantly by practice and treatment; budget for this separately if you prefer private dental care

rules.rule: NHS Entitlement If Your Visa Lapses or Expires
details: Your NHS entitlement (from IHS) is linked to your valid leave to remain — it applies from visa grant to visa expiry
If your Student Route visa expires or is curtailed and you are no longer lawfully resident in the UK, your entitlement to free NHS treatment ends immediately
Treatment after leave expires is chargeable at 150% of the national NHS tariff rate — NHS hospitals are required to identify and charge overseas visitors
Emergency treatment (immediately necessary to save life or prevent serious deterioration) will NOT be withheld, but you remain liable for the cost at 150% of NHS tariff
Urgent treatment (not immediately life-threatening but cannot safely wait) may be withheld until payment arrangements are agreed, or provided with a debt registered

CRITICAL: if your visa extension is pending but has not yet been decided and your current leave has expired, notify the NHS provider — there are specific rules for patients with outstanding applications (section 3C leave); seek advice from your university's International Student Support team immediately
To protect your NHS entitlement: ensure your Student Route visa is renewed before it expires; keep your IHS payment record; do not allow your leave to lapse
authorizationSources: NHS Overseas Visitor Charging Regulations 2015 (as amended)
gov.uk — Charging overseas visitors in England guidance

details: Register with a local GP (General Practitioner) as soon as possible after arrival — this is your primary care doctor for all non-emergency healthcare
Use the NHS 'Find a GP' service (nhs.uk/service-search/find-a-gp) to find a surgery accepting new patients near your campus
Documents to bring when registering: your passport, proof of your IHS payment (your visa grant or IHS payment reference), and your UK address
GPs cannot refuse to register you solely because of your nationality or immigration status — however, they can decline new patients if their list is full

If no GP surgery near you is accepting new patients: contact your university health centre (most universities have an on-site medical practice for students); alternatively call NHS 111 who can direct you to the nearest available surgery
Emergency treatment is available at A&E (Accident and Emergency) departments at NHS hospitals — call 999 for life-threatening emergencies
Call 111 (free) for urgent non-emergency medical advice, 24/7 — NHS 111 can also arrange emergency GP appointments if needed
Mental health support is available through the NHS — ask your GP for a referral
Most UK universities provide free, confidential counselling services for students
Samaritans: 116 123 (free, 24/7, anonymous) — emotional support and crisis listening service
Crisis text line: Text SHOUT to 85258 (UK, free, 24/7)

NHS_111.name: NHS 111
NHS_111.title: Urgent non-emergency medical advice
NHS_111.description: Free, 24/7. Use for urgent illness or injury that is not life-threatening

Emergency_Services.name: Emergency Services
Emergency_Services.description: Police, fire, ambulance — life-threatening emergencies only

Samaritans.name: Samaritans
Samaritans.title: Emotional support and crisis listening
Samaritans.description: Free, 24/7, anonymous — for anyone struggling or in distress

links.title: Immigration Health Surcharge — GOV.UK
links.description: Pay IHS and check current rates

links.title: Find a GP — NHS
links.description: Find a GP surgery near your university

complianceNotes: Do not confuse NHS (UK) with OSHC (Australia) or US private insurance — the IHS payment replaces the need for private insurance in most cases
Dental and optical care typically require separate private payment or private insurance — budget accordingly

documents.title: Cultural Adaptation and Islamic Accommodations — UK
documents.summary: The UK has a large and established Muslim community. Most UK universities have prayer facilities, halal food options, and student Islamic societies. Understanding UK academic and social norms will support academic success and wellbeing.

scope.failureImpact: Social isolation, academic difficulties, wellbeing challenges

details: Most UK universities have a multi-faith prayer room or Islamic prayer hall — check your university's Student Union or Faith Centre
Islamic Societies (IsocS) are active at virtually all UK universities — join early for community and practical support
Halal-certified food is widely available in UK cities; many university canteens label halal options or have dedicated sections
Ramadan accommodations (exam rescheduling, iftar events) are available — register with your department early in the semester
Friday Jumu'ah prayers: many universities allow short breaks around Friday prayer times — check with your department
controls: Contact your university's Islamic Society before arriving — most have WhatsApp groups for incoming students
Register religious observance needs at the start of term, not during exam periods

details: Independent thinking and critical analysis are highly valued — tutors expect you to question and evaluate sources, not just summarize them
Seminars and tutorials expect active participation — silence is often interpreted as disengagement, not respect
Office hours (called 'office hours' or 'drop-in sessions') are encouraged — use them; it is not seen as a sign of weakness
Plagiarism standards are strictly enforced — the UK uses Turnitin and similar tools; always cite sources, including when paraphrasing
Punctuality is expected for classes and appointments — arriving late without notice is considered disrespectful

ISOC.name: Islamic Society (IsocS)
ISOC.title: Muslim student community
ISOC.description: Available at virtually all UK universities via Student Union — search '[your university] Islamic Society'

Oman_Embassy_London.name: Embassy of Oman — London
Oman_Embassy_London.title: Consular services for Omani citizens in UK
Oman_Embassy_London.description: For passport renewal, consular emergencies, and student affairs

links.title: UKCISA — Student Life in the UK
links.description: Practical guide to living and studying in the UK

links.title: Embassy of Oman — London
links.description: Consular support for Omani students in the UK

documents.title: Council Tax Exemption and Renting in the UK
documents.summary: Full-time students in the UK are exempt from Council Tax, but exemption is NOT automatic — you must apply and present a certificate from your university each year. Failing to claim can result in significant debt. UK private renting also carries specific obligations: tenancy deposits must be protected, and written tenancy agreements are standard.

appliesTo: All Omani students renting privately in UK
scope.failureImpact: Council Tax debt, credit damage, difficulty renting again

rules.rule: Council Tax Exemption for Full-Time Students
details: Full-time students are completely exempt from Council Tax — you should pay nothing
Exemption is NOT applied automatically; you must inform your local council and provide a Council Tax exemption certificate from your university
Obtain the certificate from your university's student records or registry office — usually available via your student portal
Submit the certificate to your local council online or by post as soon as you move in
If all occupants of a property are full-time students, the whole property is exempt
If you live with non-students, they will still owe Council Tax but your share is disregarded
Renew your exemption certificate each academic year — councils require annual confirmation

commonViolations.text: Not claiming exemption and ignoring council tax bills
commonViolations.consequence: Debt registered, enforcement action, court summons, bailiffs

commonViolations.text: Forgetting to renew exemption certificate at the start of each academic year

controls: Claim exemption within the first two weeks of moving in
Keep a copy of your exemption certificate and the council's acknowledgment
Set a reminder to renew in September each year

details: Landlords must protect your deposit in a government-approved Tenancy Deposit Scheme (TDS, mydeposits, or DPS) within 30 days of receipt
Deposit cap: landlords cannot charge more than 5 weeks' rent as a deposit (for annual rents under £50,000)
You must receive a signed tenancy agreement — never pay a deposit without one
Landlords must provide: a gas safety certificate, an electrical installation condition report, an EPC (energy performance certificate), and the government's 'How to Rent' guide
Landlords cannot evict you without a legal process — do not leave due to verbal pressure alone
Report disrepair in writing to your landlord; local councils can act against landlords who fail to maintain properties
controls: Check your deposit has been protected — landlord must give you a deposit protection certificate
Photograph the property on arrival and departure to document condition
Use Shelter's online checker (shelter.org.uk) to verify your rights if a dispute arises

details: A property is an HMO if 3 or more unrelated people share facilities (bathroom, kitchen)
Landlords of HMOs must hold a licence from the local council
HMO licence means the property meets minimum room size and fire safety standards
If your landlord's HMO is unlicensed, report to the local council — you may be entitled to rent repayment
UK landlords must check that all adult tenants have the right to rent in the UK before the tenancy begins
Your eVisa (Student Route) gives you the right to rent — share the online share code with your landlord via gov.uk/view-prove-immigration-status
If landlord asks for a physical BRP, explain that from July 2025 BRPs are no longer issued — your eVisa share code is the correct proof

Shelter.name: Shelter
Shelter.title: Housing and tenancy advice charity
Shelter.description: Free housing advice including tenant rights, deposit disputes, and eviction

Citizens_Advice.name: Citizens Advice
Citizens_Advice.title: Free legal, financial and housing advice
Citizens_Advice.description: Free, independent advice on any issue including housing, debt, and employment

links.title: Council Tax for students — GOV.UK
links.description: Official guidance on who must pay and who is exempt

links.title: Tenancy Deposit Protection — GOV.UK
links.description: Verify your deposit is protected and learn your rights

links.title: Private renting rights — GOV.UK
links.description: Overview of tenant rights and landlord obligations

complianceNotes: University accommodation (halls of residence) is usually already council-tax exempt — contact your university if unsure
Council Tax exemption does not reduce your liability if you work full-time after withdrawing from your course — status change triggers liability
MoHE does not cover Council Tax separately — it is expected to come from your living allowance; budget for this even if claiming exemption, in case of administrative delays

documents.title: UK Tax, National Insurance, and HMRC Obligations
documents.summary: Students working in the UK are subject to UK income tax and National Insurance (NI) contributions. You need a National Insurance number before or shortly after starting work. Most employees are taxed through PAYE — tax is deducted automatically by your employer. The annual personal allowance for 2025/26 is £12,570; income below this threshold is not taxed. MoHE scholarships and living allowances are generally not classed as taxable income in the UK.

appliesTo: Omani students working in UK under Student Route
scope.complianceTier: Legal Mandatory (for those with employment income)
scope.failureImpact: Tax debt, HMRC investigation, penalties, issues with future UK visa applications

details: A National Insurance (NI) number is required to work legally and pay the correct tax and NI contributions
Apply for a NI number online via gov.uk/apply-national-insurance-number — you can work while your application is pending, but give your employer your NI number as soon as it arrives
NI numbers are personal and permanent — keep it safe, never share it unnecessarily
Employers must not refuse to employ you while your NI number application is pending if you can otherwise prove your right to work
PAYE is the UK system where your employer deducts income tax and NI directly from your salary before you are paid
You will receive a payslip each pay period showing gross pay, deductions, and net pay
At the end of the tax year (5 April), your employer provides a P60 — keep this as it proves your earnings and tax paid
When you leave a job, you receive a P45 — give this to your next employer so they apply the correct tax code
If you are taxed too much (common for students who work part of the year), HMRC will usually refund automatically or you can claim via gov.uk/claim-tax-refund
Personal Allowance: £12,570 per year — income up to this amount is not taxed
Basic rate (20%): taxable income from £12,571 to £50,270
Higher rate (40%): taxable income above £50,270
Most student part-time workers earn well below the personal allowance and pay little or no income tax
National Insurance: employees pay 8% on earnings between £12,570 and £50,270 per year (Employee Class 1 NI)
If you are self-employed (on the Graduate Route, not the Student Route), you must register with HMRC for Self Assessment and file a tax return annually
Also required if you earn income above £1,000 from freelance work, rental income, or have multiple income sources
Register via gov.uk/register-for-self-assessment by 5 October following the end of the tax year in which you became self-employed
Self-employed individuals also pay Class 2 and Class 4 National Insurance contributions
IMPORTANT: Self-employment is NOT permitted on the Student Route — only on the Graduate Route or other post-study visas

rules.rule: MoHE Scholarship Income and UK Tax
details: MoHE scholarships (tuition payments made directly to the university) are not taxable in the UK — they are not income
Living allowances paid by MoHE are generally not taxable as they are classified as maintenance grants rather than employment income
However, if MoHE pays you for research services or consulting, that income may be taxable — seek advice from your university's tax advisor or HMRC
There is a Double Taxation Agreement between Oman and the UK — in most cases you will not be taxed twice on the same income
authorizationSources: HMRC guidance on scholarships and grants

HMRC.name: HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC)
HMRC.title: UK tax authority
HMRC.description: For tax queries, NI number issues, Self Assessment, and overpayment refunds

links.title: Apply for a National Insurance Number — GOV.UK
links.description: Apply online — do this as soon as you start looking for work

links.title: Income tax rates and allowances — GOV.UK
links.description: Current personal allowance and tax band thresholds

links.title: Check if you've paid too much tax — GOV.UK
links.description: Claim a tax refund if PAYE over-deducted

links.title: UKCISA — Taxation for International Students
links.description: Plain-English guide to UK tax for international students

complianceNotes: Never give your NI number to third parties claiming to process jobs or visas on your behalf — this is a common scam targeting international students
Keep all payslips, P60s, and P45s for at least 6 years — HMRC can investigate this far back
Your university's international student office or student union often provides free tax advice sessions — use them before filing any return

documents.title: Travel and Re-Entry to the UK on the Student Route
documents.summary: Travelling internationally on the Student Route (UK) is simpler than the F-1 system — there is no travel signature required on a physical document. From July 2025, you use an eVisa and share your immigration status digitally. However, your passport must be valid, absence from the UK during term may violate your attendance obligations, and long absences can trigger sponsor reporting duties.

appliesTo: All Omani students on Student Route visa travelling internationally
scope.failureImpact: Refusal of re-entry, leave curtailment, sponsor reporting to UKVI

details: From July 2025, Student Route grants are issued as digital eVisas — there is no physical stamp, vignette, or BRP card
Before travelling, generate a 'share code' via gov.uk/view-prove-immigration-status and note it or share it with airlines when required
Most airlines check your UK immigration status electronically via the Home Office API — you may not need to provide a share code manually, but have one ready
UK Border Force at the airport can verify your status electronically — you do not need to print anything
If you have biometric enrolment, you can use eGates at UK airports for faster entry
Always log in to your UKVI account before travelling to confirm your eVisa is active and correctly recorded
controls: Check your eVisa status before every trip, especially if your passport has changed
Link your new passport to your UKVI account immediately if you renew your Omani passport
Note your eVisa expiry date — re-entry is refused if leave has expired even if your course is ongoing

details: Your passport must be valid to travel — UK immigration does not require 6 months' passport validity beyond your return date (unlike some countries), but airlines may have their own rules
Your eVisa is linked to your passport number — if you renew your passport, you must update your UKVI account immediately via the online service
Failure to link your new passport to your UKVI account can result in denial of boarding or refusal of re-entry
Omani passports are renewed at the Omani Embassy in London — allow 4-6 weeks and do not travel with an expired passport
failureModes: Renewing passport without updating UKVI account — airlines cannot verify your immigration status
Travelling on an expired Omani passport

details: There is no formal 'travel signature' requirement on the Student Route — unlike the US F-1, you do not need your DSO to sign anything before travel
At the UK border, your leave to remain is verified electronically against your passport biometrics and eVisa

If you are asked questions at the border about your studies or sponsor, answer honestly — carry your university enrolment confirmation or a letter from your International Student Office as supporting evidence
If you are travelling during term time, be prepared to explain your attendance arrangements — have contact details for your International Student Support team
Travelling during term time is not prohibited but you remain bound by your course attendance requirements — missing classes without authorization can trigger sponsor reporting
Notify your department and International Student Support team before any term-time travel exceeding a few days
Sponsors must report students who are absent for more than 28 days without authorization — extended travel home during term can trigger this
Extended absence from the UK (over a few weeks) may require your sponsor to notify UKVI, which can affect your leave
Travel during official university vacations is unrestricted — plan holidays during vacation periods where possible
The UK and Ireland share a Common Travel Area (CTA) — you can cross between England, Scotland, Wales, Northern Ireland, and the Republic of Ireland without border checks in most cases
However, your Student Route visa is UK-only — it does not give you the right to live or work in Ireland
If you travel from Ireland into the UK via a route that triggers a border check, your eVisa will be verified normally

links.title: UKCISA — Travelling and Returning to the UK
links.description: Practical guide to travel for students on the Student Route

links.title: Embassy of Oman — London (Consular Services)
links.description: Passport renewal and emergency consular support for Omani students in the UK

complianceNotes: If you have been outside the UK for a total of 6 months or more in any 12-month period, this can affect future applications for Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) — relevant if you intend to settle in UK long-term
Re-entry is not guaranteed — even with a valid eVisa, Border Force officers have discretion. Carry your university enrollment letter and course details when returning from abroad
If your visa expires while you are abroad and you cannot return in time, contact UKVI immediately — do not try to re-enter on an expired visa

documents.title: Academic Integrity and Misconduct — UK Universities
documents.summary: UK universities enforce strict academic integrity policies. Plagiarism, collusion, contract cheating, and unauthorized use of AI can result in failed assignments, failed modules, suspension, or expulsion. Contract cheating (paying for essays or using essay mills) has been illegal in England, Wales, and Northern Ireland since 2022. AI use policies vary by university and assessment — always check before submitting.

appliesTo: All Omani students enrolled at UK universities
scope.failureImpact: Failed assessment, failed module, academic misconduct hearing, suspension, expulsion, degree withholding, future visa implications

details: Plagiarism means presenting someone else's words, ideas, data, or work as your own without proper attribution
This includes: copying from books or articles without citation, paraphrasing too closely without citation, using a classmate's work, and copying from websites without referencing
Self-plagiarism (reusing your own previous assessed work without disclosure) is also prohibited
Turnitin similarity reports are used at most UK universities — a high similarity score triggers manual review; percentage alone does not determine guilt
You are responsible for understanding what plagiarism means before submitting — ignorance is not an accepted defence
authorizationSources: Your university's academic integrity policy (check your student handbook)
controls: Cite every source — in text and in your bibliography — using your department's required citation style (Harvard, APA, OSCOLA, etc.)
Use your university's plagiarism checker (often Turnitin) to review your own work before submission
When in doubt, cite it — over-referencing is never penalized

details: Collusion is unauthorized collaboration on an individually assessed piece of work
Discussing ideas in a study group is encouraged; copying or sharing answers is not
You must not share draft assignments with classmates if the work is individual — even if your intention is to help
Group work must only be submitted as group work — submitting joint work as individual work is collusion
Contract cheating means paying a third party (essay mill, homework help site, tutor) to produce work you then submit as your own

The Higher Education (Freedom of Speech) Act 2023 and preceding legislation make it a criminal offence in England, Wales, and Northern Ireland for essay mills to provide these services — penalties apply to providers
Students submitting contracted work face severe academic penalties including expulsion
This includes: using essay-writing websites, paying for completed problem sets, and having a tutor write your assignment
Ghostwritten dissertations or theses are treated as the most serious form of academic misconduct
UK universities have diverse AI use policies — policies vary by course, assessment type, and university
Many universities prohibit AI-generated text in essays, dissertations, and exams unless explicitly permitted
Some assessments permit AI as a tool (e.g., literature search, coding assistance) but require disclosure
Using AI without disclosure where prohibited is treated as academic misconduct equivalent to plagiarism
AI-detection tools (e.g., Turnitin's AI writing detection) are used at many institutions
ALWAYS check your module or course handbook for the specific AI policy before starting any assessed work
authorizationSources: Your university's AI use in assessment policy (published per-module or in student handbook)
controls: Read the assessment brief carefully — look for any statement about permitted AI use
When AI is permitted, document how you used it (many universities require an AI use statement in the submission)
Contact your module leader or personal tutor if you are unsure whether a specific AI use is acceptable

details: If suspected misconduct is identified, your university will initiate a formal academic misconduct investigation
You will receive written notice and be given the opportunity to respond in writing and/or attend a hearing
Penalties (in increasing severity): formal warning, mark reduction, failed assessment, failed module, suspension, expulsion
Serious misconduct (especially dissertation fraud) may result in a degree being withheld or rescinded even after graduation
Academic misconduct findings may be disclosed on academic transcripts and in references
UKVI may be informed if expulsion results from misconduct — this can affect your immigration status
mitigation: If you receive a misconduct allegation letter, contact your Students' Union for free advice and representation before responding
Do not panic or admit to anything in writing before seeking advice — the process allows you to state your case
Contact your International Student Support team immediately — misconduct findings affecting enrolment have immigration consequences

Students_Union.name: Students' Union Advice Service
Students_Union.title: Free, confidential, independent advice and representation
Students_Union.description: Available at every UK university Students' Union — contact them first if you face any academic misconduct allegation

links.title: UKCISA — Academic integrity
links.description: Overview of UK academic expectations for international students

complianceNotes: Cultural differences in citation practices mean some students arrive unfamiliar with Western referencing norms — many universities offer free academic writing workshops; attend these in your first semester
Using WhatsApp group chats to share assignment answers or problem-set solutions, even among friends, can constitute collusion — be cautious
Turnitin compares your submission against billions of web pages, journal articles, and previously submitted student work — unique phrasing alone does not guarantee a pass

documents.title: Banking, Money Management, and Financial Setup — UK
documents.summary: Opening a UK bank account as an international student can be challenging — traditional banks require proof of UK address, which students often lack on arrival. Digital banks (Monzo, Starling, Revolut) offer fast account opening with minimal documentation and are widely accepted for salary payments and direct debits. Understanding budgeting for UK living costs is essential for MoHE-sponsored students managing their living allowance.

appliesTo: All Omani students arriving in UK
scope.failureImpact: Inability to receive salary, pay rent, or access benefits of UK financial system

details: Traditional UK banks (Barclays, HSBC, NatWest, Lloyds, Santander) offer student accounts but typically require: proof of UK address (tenancy agreement, utility bill), a valid passport, proof of student status (CAS or university letter)
The main difficulty: proof of address is required before you have an address — apply as soon as you have a tenancy agreement or university accommodation letter
HSBC and Santander have dedicated international student banking schemes that can be applied for before arrival via your university partnership — check if your university has a partner bank
Digital banks are the fastest option on arrival: Monzo, Starling, and Revolut allow account opening with just your passport and a selfie — no UK address required immediately
Digital bank accounts are widely accepted by employers for salary payments and by landlords for standing order rent
A UK bank account with a sort code and account number is required by most employers — set one up in your first week
authorizationSources: Individual bank policies (check your bank's website for current requirements)
controls: Apply for a Monzo or Starling account from your phone in the first few days — both are free and take 5-10 minutes
Then apply for a traditional student bank account once you have a UK address — traditional accounts have overdraft facilities useful for managing cash flow
Never share your account details or online banking credentials with anyone

details: MoHE living allowances are typically paid monthly or quarterly — confirm the payment schedule before departure
MoHE usually transfers allowances in Omani Rial (OMR) or to a nominated account — confirm the transfer method and expected dates to avoid arriving with insufficient funds
Currency conversion from OMR to GBP: exchange rates fluctuate — receiving your allowance in a multi-currency account (Wise, Revolut) can reduce conversion fees versus traditional bank transfers
Build a 4-week emergency cash buffer: if a transfer is delayed, you need funds to cover rent and food
Typical monthly UK student living costs: London £1,400-£2,000; outside London £900-£1,400 (including rent, food, transport, phone — not tuition)
authorizationSources: Bank of England exchange rate data
controls: Confirm with MoHE the exact transfer timeline before you leave Oman
Bring enough cash or a prepaid travel card to cover at least 4 weeks of expenses on arrival
Set up a Wise or Revolut account to receive OMR and convert to GBP at interbank rates

details: Sending money between Oman and UK: use Wise (formerly TransferWise) or a specialist money transfer service for better exchange rates than banks
Bank-to-bank international wire transfers (SWIFT) typically take 3-5 business days and carry fees of £10-30 per transfer
Western Union and MoneyGram are available but typically have higher fees for student amounts
Wise transfers are usually cheaper and faster (often 1-2 days) for OMR to GBP conversions
Notify your UK bank if you are expecting a large international transfer — banks may freeze accounts if large unexpected transfers arrive
HMRC phone and email scams: HMRC does NOT call students demanding immediate payment or threatening arrest — these are scams. Hang up.
Visa fee scams: fraudulent 'visa extension' services charge fees for services that are free or cheaper direct from UKVI — always use gov.uk for visa applications
Landlord deposit scams: never pay a deposit without viewing the property in person or via a trusted agent, and never pay cash without a receipt
Cryptocurrency scams: no legitimate employer, landlord, or government agency will ask for payment in cryptocurrency
If you think you have been scammed: report to Action Fraud (actionfraud.police.uk) and your bank immediately

links.title: Monzo — Digital bank account
links.description: Open a UK bank account from your phone in minutes — no UK address required on signup

links.title: Starling Bank — Digital bank account
links.description: Free UK current account with easy international student onboarding

links.title: Wise — International money transfers
links.description: Low-fee international transfers at real exchange rates — widely used by international students

links.title: UKCISA — Money and Banking
links.description: Overview of money management for international students in UK

complianceNotes: MoHE requires you to maintain a bank account in your own name for allowance disbursement — joint accounts may not be accepted
Some UK student bank accounts offer 0% interest overdrafts — useful for cash-flow gaps between allowance payments, but must be repaid on graduation
UK banks are required to report suspicious transactions — large unexplained cash deposits can trigger an investigation, which can affect your banking access

links: Omani Ministry of Higher Education: https://www.mohe.gov.om
Embassy of Oman in London: https://www.omanembassylondon.com
emergencyContacts: Shout Crisis Text Line: Text SHOUT to 85258
